data.title: Stevie Wonder's Family Lists Longtime Calabasas Home for $15 Million — WSJ
data.description:
By Katherine Clarke

It was the acoustics that made Stevie Wonder fall in love with the house.

When the singer first saw the Los Angeles-area property about 25 years ago, his famed voice reverberated through the high-ceilinged home, according to his then-wife, clothing designer Kai Milla-Morris.

Wonder surprised her by buying the house in celebrity-studded Calabasas. "He says, 'I want to show you something,'" Milla-Morris recalled. "He had already purchased the house." Luckily, she loved the nearly 3-acre estate, and has lived there ever since; Wonder moved out after their 2012 divorce.

Now that their two sons are grown, Milla-Morris said, she is putting the property on the market for $15 million.

Located in a gated community, the six-bedroom property has 11,120 square feet of living space and a pool.

Milla-Morris married the "Isn't She Lovely?" singer in the Bahamas in 2000. Wonder, whose real name is Stevland Hardaway Morris, declined to comment. The 74-year-old musician has since remarried, but he and Milla-Morris remain close friends, she said.

Milla-Morris moved to Los Angeles from the East Coast after she married Wonder, and the couple lived at the Peninsula Hotel in Beverly Hills for close to a year while they searched for a home. They paid $2.85 million for the modern Mediterranean-style home in 2000, property records show.

"It just had this incredible, timeless architecture and breathtaking surroundings," Milla-Morris said. "It was on its own little hill and had lots of space."

Once they moved in, Milla-Morris said, there was always music around the house. "Steve, even the incredible musician as he is, he practiced every single day," she said. Her children also played the piano, and "the family was always having great jam sessions."

The home has a music area off the living room, where Milla-Morris keeps Wonder's rare Bösendorfer piano. It is not included in the sale. "We have several pianos throughout the house that the kids learned on," she said. "Once they learned how to play, then they started playing on the Bösendorfer."

The home, she said, has also been a gathering place for their wider, blended family, including Wonder's kids from other relationships.

Milla-Morris loved throwing epic birthday parties at the house. One Coachella-themed party had a Ferris wheel and food trucks. For another event, they had a harmonica-playing elephant; the same elephant starred in the movie "Ace Ventura: Pet Detective," she said.

Wonder and Milla-Morris didn't do much to the house, built in the 1990s, when they first moved in. "As a young bride and a mother, I had no interest in doing anything to the house," Milla-Morris said. "I had to let them be kids. They're going to mark the wall, put fingerprints everywhere. Just forget about it."

But her sons, Kailand and Mandla Morris, are now 19 and 23, and Milla-Morris spent at least $3 million upgrading the property during the pandemic. She remodeled the primary suite, dividing it into separate areas using a double-sided fireplace. The "vignettes," as she called them, allow the space to feel more intimate. She gut renovated the kitchen, opening it up to the rest of the living space and bringing in new finishes, like an oversize marble island crafted from two large pieces of book-matched marble. The property also has a formal dining room and a family room with a wine-storage area and bar.

Milla-Morris said she feels it is time to sell the property, which will also be listed as a rental for $65,000 a month.

"It's very big," she said. "I feel like this is the right time to venture out into a new space."

The property is listed by Branden Williams, Rayni Williams, Dennis DeWalt and Brandon Meneses of the Beverly Hills Estates.

Write to Katherine Clarke at Katherine.Clarke@wsj.com

data.title: Utilities Down, But not by Much, on Safe-Haven Demand — Utilities Roundup
data.description:
Shares of power producers fell, finishing flat on the week, amid defensive demand.

The utilities industry group has held up better than the broad market during the recent correction, as it stands to gain from the slide in Treasury yields and an appetite for safe havens.

Investors are retreating from cyclical sectors due to the growing uncertainty about the outlook for growth and inflation cited by global central banks, including the Federal Reserve.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

Levi & Korsinsky Announces the Filing of a Securities Class Action on Behalf of ModivCare Inc. (MODV) Shareholders
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / March 21, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your ModivCare Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/modivcare-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=137906&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=1003885&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2frKM2z0RiBKo" width="0"/></div></div></div><p><strong>THE LAWSUIT:</strong> A class action securities lawsuit was filed against ModivCare Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between November 3, 2022 and September 15, 2024.</p><p><strong>CASE DETAILS:</strong> According to the filed complaint, defendants made false statements and/or concealed that certain contracts used in ModivCare's NEMT segment caused the Company's free cash flow to deteriorate and as a result, (1) contract renegotiations and pricing accommodations negatively impacted the Company's adjusted EBITDA; (2) the Company had insufficient liquidity; and (3) defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis.</p><p><strong>WHAT'S NEXT?</strong> If you suffered a loss in ModivCare stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to <a href="https://pr.report/8l7n" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/pslra-1/modivcare-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=137906&wire=1</a> to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. <strong>There is no cost or obligation to participate.</strong></p><p><strong>WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY:</strong> Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.</p><p><strong>CONTACT:</strong><br/>Levi & Korsinsky, LLP<br/>Joseph E. data.title: Golden Ocean Group: Board Intends to Maintain Current Dividend Policy
Haun Ventures seeks $1 billion to fund crypto startups: report
data.description:
Haun Ventures, founded by Andreessen Horowitz alumna Katie Haun, is raising $1 billion to launch two new crypto-focused funds. When closed, the raise, according to Fortune, would mark the largest venture financing deal since the beginning of President Trump’s second term. 

The funding round is expected to close in June. Haun will split the capital 50/50, investing $500 million in early-stage projects and $500 million in late-stage projects. This is similar to Haun’s founding capital of $1.5 billion, which was raised in 2022 ahead of the crypto market decline. 

Haun Ventures was started amid a wave of mega raises by crypto firms, including a record-setting $4.5 billion raise by a16z Crypto, a financial backer of Haun. Other heavy-hitting VCs — Polychain and Paradigm — are also investors in Haun. 

This most recent funding round comes amid a thawing of the regulatory freeze around crypto, given Trump’s open support of the industry. On Thursday, Trump reiterated his commitment to making the U.S. the crypto capital of the world.

Katie Haun previously served as a Coinbase board member and general partner at a16z. She retired from Coinbase last year. Before that, she was a prosecutor for the Justice Department, where she helped litigate the case against Silk Road founder Ross Ulbricht. 

Haun Ventures initially planned to deploy its first $1.5 billion over a two-year period, though it took a more cautious approach following the collapse of FTX in late 2022. By the middle of 2023, the firm was still sitting on about 60% of its capital. Haun’s notable investments include NFT firm Zora and stablecoin protocol Bridge, which Stripe recently acquired for $1.1 billion.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

© 2025 The Block. All Rights Reserved. This article is provided for informational purposes only. It is not offered or intended to be used as legal, tax, investment, financial, or other advice.

data.title: Mustang Energy Engages Axiom Exploration for Airborne Geophysical Survey over the Spur Project
data.description:
VANCOUVER, British Columbia, March 21, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Mustang Energy Corp. (CSE: MEC, OTC:MECPF, FRA:92T) (the “Company” or “Mustang”) is pleased to announce that it has engaged Axiom Exploration Group (“Axiom”) to conduct an Aerial Electromagnetic Survey (Xcite™ TDEM) with Radiometrics over its Spur Project located south of the Athabasca Basin, Saskatchewan (the “Spur Project”).

The Company anticipates that the Xcite™ TDEM survey with Radiometrics will commence in early May 2025 and encompass the portion of the claim package which has not been covered by modern day electromagnetic (EM) survey techniques. The airborne survey aims to precisely locate EM and radiometric anomalies, and aid in geological interpretations on the property advancing exploration targeting.

In addition, Mustang has applied for exploration permits on the Spur Project and the Company’s 914W Project. The permit applications include ground based mineral exploration activities such as trail construction, line-cutting, ground geophysical surveys, and diamond drilling. Subject to obtaining the required permits and approvals, Mustang plans to initiate exploration activities aimed at further assessing the viability of these projects, as well as to continue consultation with local communities.

About Xcite™ TDEM

Xcite™ is a new generation of helicopter-borne time-domain electromagnetic (TDEM) systems developed by New Resolution Geophysics (NRG™). Axiom is an exclusive provider of this service in North America. This state-of-the-art technology provides an efficient alternative to prior TDEM technologies for mineral exploration. The system utilizes a patented inflatable transmitter loop with a diameter of approximately 20 meters, suspended about 30 meters below the helicopter. The Xcite™ system offers improved signal clarity, enabling the detection of subtle subsurface features. It features a programmable waveform with a fast turn-off time, allowing for flexibility in data acquisition and improved resolution of both shallow and deep targets.

https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/11699211-1f81-4e70-bf12-4f39172160e4

About the Spur Project

The Spur Project comprises nine claims covering 17,929 hectares. It is located just south of the Athabasca Basin in northern Saskatchewan, a region globally renowned for its high-grade uranium deposits. The property is approximately 40 kilometers southeast of Cameco’s Key Lake Mill Operation and 20 kilometers southwest of Skyharbour Resources/Terra Clean Energy’s Fraser Lakes Zone B deposit (Figure 1). The Spur Project’s geological setting combines Proterozoic basement rocks of the Wollaston Group and uraniferous pegmatites, presenting potential for uranium, thorium and rare earth element (REE) exploration. The Spur Project offers lower cost exploration due to the absence of Athabasca Sandstone cover. Adjacent Properties host high-grade surface grab samples including the Pipe Lake Showing with up to 8.0% U₃O₈ (sample # R69-10)2 within a hornblende-rich pegmatite 2.5 km northeast of the Spur Project boundary. The Red October Showing, east of the project boundary, shows grab sample assays of up to 1.93% U (sample # JBELR063)3 within syenite, and an outcrop chip sample through semipelitic gneiss showing 1.34% U (sample # AGELR008)3 over 1 meter.

About the 914W Project

The 914W Project consists of one claim covering 1,260 hectares. It is situated approximately 48 km southwest of Cameco’s Key Lake Operation, offering favourable logistics and access via Highway 914. The 914W Project is positioned within the Western Wollaston Domain, known for unconformity/basement hosted uranium mineralization. The 914W Project hosts favorable geology with local graphite bearing assemblages. Immediately to the north of the 914W property is the Scurry Rainbow Zone E4 and the Don Lake Trenches5, where up to 1,288 ppm U was encountered in drill hole ML-11, and surface prospecting revealed up to 0.64% U3O8 in a trench at Don Lake Zone E4. While historical exploration conducted several geophysical and geological surveys over portions of the property, most of the 914W Project remains underexplored. Mustang sees notable potential for advancing uranium and rare earth element (REE) exploration on this property.

References:

  • Saskatchewan GeoAtlas, https://gisappl.saskatchewan.ca/Html5Ext/index.html?viewer=GeoAtlas
  • Saskatchewan Mineral Deposits Index. SMDI# 1005, https://applications.saskatchewan.ca/Apps/ECON_Apps/dbsearch/MinDepositQuery/default.aspx?ID=1961 
  • Saskatchewan Mineral Deposits Index. SMDI# 5219, https://applications.saskatchewan.ca/Apps/ECON_Apps/dbsearch/MinDepositQuery/default.aspx?ID=5219

Qualifying Statement

The scientific and technical information in this release has been reviewed and approved by Lynde Guillaume, P.Geo., Technical Advisor for Mustang Energy, a registered member of the Professional Engineers and Geoscientists of Saskatchewan. Ms. Guillaume is a Qualified Person as defined by National Instrument 43-101.

Adjacent Property Disclaimer:

This news release includes references with respect to uranium occurrences which are located near the Spur Project and 914W Project, including the Pipe Lake Showing, Red October Showing, Scurry Rainbow Zone E, and Don Lake Zone E. The Company advises that, notwithstanding their proximity of location, discoveries of minerals on nearby properties and any promising results thereof are not necessarily indicative of the mineralization of, or located on the Spur Project and 914W Project, or the Company’s ability to commercially exploit the Spur Project or 914W Project, or to locate any commercially exploitable deposits therefrom. The Company cautions investors on relying on this information as the Company has not confirmed the accuracy or reliability of the information.

About Mustang Energy Corp.:

Mustang is a resource exploration company focused on acquiring and developing high-potential uranium and critical mineral assets. The Company is actively exploring its properties in Northern Saskatchewan, Canada and holds 77,318 hectares in around the Athabasca Basin. Mustang's flagship property, Ford Lake, covers 7,743 hectares in the prolific eastern Athabasca Basin, while its Cigar Lake East and Roughrider South projects span 3,442 hectares to the north and the Spur Project to the south covering 17,929 hectares. Mustang has also established its footprint in the Cluff Lake region of the Athabasca Basin with the Yellowstone Project (21,820 hectares) and further expanded its presence in the south central region of the Athabasca Basin with the Dutton Project (7,633 hectares).

For further information, please contact:

Mustang Energy Corp.

Attention: Nicholas Luksha, CEO and Director

Phone: (604) 838-0184

Neither the CSE nor the Market Regulator (as that term is defined in the policies of the CSE) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

This information and these statements, referred to herein as “forward‐looking statements”, are not historical facts, are made as of the date of this news release and include without limitation, statements regarding discussions of future plans, estimates and forecasts and statements as to management's expectations and intentions with respect to, among other things, the future potential of the mineral claims held by the Company, including the Spur Project and 914W Project; the granting of exploration permits on the Spur Project and 914W Project; the timing for the commencement of the Xcite™ TDEM survey; and the potential capabilities of the Xcite™ TDEM survey. In making the forward-looking statements in this news release, the Company has applied several material assumptions, including without limitation the assumption that the Company will be able to continue exploring its properties given various environmental and economic factors outside of its control and that the results of the Xcite™ TDEM survey will provide the anticipated insights. data.title: Spark I Acquisition Corp SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Spark I Acquisition Corp, a blank check company focused on mergers and acquisitions, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business activities, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces as it seeks to complete its initial business combination.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $3.15 million for the year ended December 31, 2024, primarily driven by interest income on investments held in the trust account.
  • Net Loss: $0.73 million for the period ended December 31, 2023, due to operating costs and administration fees, partially offset by interest income.
  • Operating Costs: $0.85 million incurred for organizational activities and IPO preparation.
  • Administration Fees - Related Party: $1.25 million as part of ongoing administrative expenses.
  • Interest Earned on Investments Held in the Trust Account: $5.25 million, significantly contributing to the net income for the year ended December 31, 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Business Model and Strategy: Spark I Acquisition Corp is focused on effecting mergers, share exchanges, asset acquisitions, share purchases, reorganizations, or similar combinations with businesses or assets, particularly late-stage technology startups in Asia or U.S. technology companies with a strong Asia presence, excluding those with principal operations in China.
  • Initial Public Offering (IPO): Completed on October 11, 2023, offering 10,000,000 units, each consisting of one Class A ordinary share and one-half of one redeemable warrant, raising $100,500,000 placed in a trust account.
  • Private Placement: Conducted simultaneously with the IPO, resulting in the purchase of 8,490,535 warrants by the Sponsor, generating proceeds of $8,490,535.
  • Business Combination Efforts: Actively seeking a business combination, having signed a non-binding letter of intent with Kneron Holding Corporation, a provider of edge AI solutions, and another company in the hospitality software space. Negotiations with Kneron are ongoing despite the expiration of the LOIs.
  • Market Opportunity: Leveraging connections with SparkLabs Group to identify acquisition targets, focusing on companies with positive inflection points due to disruptive strategies and business models.
  • Future Outlook: Aiming to complete a business combination by July 11, 2025, exploring opportunities to support the merged company in becoming successful in public markets. The management team is focused on identifying targets that align with the company's strategic goals and market opportunities.
  • Operational Focus: Prioritizing targets with compelling business models, proven track records, significant growth potential, unrecognized value, and experienced management teams. The SPAC structure is seen as an ideal vehicle to bring these companies to public markets.
  • Management and Governance: Led by a management team with significant experience in capital markets, investment, and entrepreneurship, supported by SparkLabs Group's extensive network and resources.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: Actively pursuing an initial business combination, having signed a non-binding LOI with Kneron, a provider of edge AI solutions, and negotiating terms for a binding agreement. The company has also assessed potential targets within the SparkLabs Group ecosystem.
  • Capital Management: Completed its IPO on October 11, 2023, raising $100.5 million, which was placed in a trust account. Issued a promissory note for up to $1.9 million to its Sponsor to finance working capital needs. The company has not paid any dividends and does not plan to do so before completing its initial business combination.
  • Future Outlook: Aiming to consummate a business combination by July 11, 2025, focusing on negotiating a binding agreement with Kneron. Faces liquidity challenges and substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern if it fails to complete a business combination by the deadline.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operational Risks: As a recently incorporated entity with no operating history or revenues, the company faces significant risks in achieving its business objectives. The management team's past performance may not be indicative of future success, and there is substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern due to financial constraints and the need for additional capital.
  • Shareholder Risks: Public shareholders may not have the opportunity to vote on the proposed initial business combination, which could proceed even without majority shareholder support. The ability of shareholders to redeem their shares for cash could make the company's financial condition unattractive to potential business combination targets.
  • Regulatory Risks: Potential imposition of a 1% U.S. federal excise tax on redemptions of ordinary shares if the initial business combination involves a U.S. company. The SEC's new rules regulating SPACs could increase costs and time needed to complete a business combination.
  • Market Risks: Global financial volatility, geopolitical tensions, and economic uncertainties could adversely affect the company's search for a business combination and the performance of any acquired business. The company also faces competition from other entities seeking similar business opportunities.
  • Nasdaq Delisting: The possibility of Nasdaq delisting the company's securities would limit investors' ability to trade and could subject the company to additional restrictions.
  • Financing Challenges: The company may face challenges in obtaining additional financing to complete a business combination or fund operations, potentially leading to restructuring or abandonment of a business combination.
  • Management Strategies: Seeking shareholder approval for an extension of the business combination deadline if necessary, and potentially liquidating the trust account to hold funds in cash to avoid being deemed an investment company under the Investment Company Act. However, these strategies may not fully mitigate the risks, and the company may be forced to liquidate if unable to complete a business combination by the deadline.

SEC Filing: Spark I Acquisition Corp [ SPKL ] - 10-K - Mar. 21, 2025

data.title: Melar Acquisition Corp. I/Cayman SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Melar Acquisition Corp. I/Cayman, a blank check company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, has released its Form 10-K report, detailing its financial and operational performance for the fiscal year. The company, focused on effecting a Business Combination in the emerging finance sector, including retail finance, specialty finance, and financial technology, has provided insights into its financial health, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $4.21 million, primarily driven by dividend and interest income on marketable securities and cash held in the Trust Account.
  • Dividend and Interest Income on Marketable Securities and Cash Held in the Trust Account: $4.41 million, contributing significantly to the net income.
  • Unrealized Gain on Over-Allotment Liability: $0.17 million, offset by general and administrative costs.
  • General and Administrative Costs: $0.37 million, incurred as a result of being a public company and due diligence expenses.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: The company is a blank check company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, focused on effecting a Business Combination in the emerging finance sector, including retail finance, specialty finance, and financial technology.
  • Initial Public Offering: On June 20, 2024, the company completed its Initial Public Offering, raising $160 million through the sale of 16,000,000 Units, each consisting of one Public Share and one-half of one Public Warrant.
  • Management Team: The management team, led by Gautam Ivatury, Edward Lifshitz, and Eric Lifshitz, has extensive experience in emerging finance sectors, including retail finance, specialty finance, renewable energy financing, and financial technology.
  • Market Overview: The emerging finance sector is experiencing growth due to advancements in financial technology, with significant opportunities in digital lending and fintech markets, which are expected to grow substantially over the next few years.
  • Target Sectors: The company intends to focus on sectors such as MSME finance, specialty finance, renewable energy financing, and digital payments, which are poised for growth due to unmet financing needs and technological advancements.
  • Business Combination Criteria: The company seeks targets with substantial growth opportunities, leadership positions, profitability, public company readiness, and strong management teams, with an enterprise value between $400 million and $1.2 billion.
  • Sourcing of Targets: The management team leverages a broad network of contacts and corporate relationships to identify potential Business Combination targets, focusing on proprietary deal flow and strategic alliances.
  • Redemption Rights: Public Shareholders have the opportunity to redeem their shares upon completion of the initial Business Combination, with specific conditions and limitations outlined in the company's charter.
  • Future Outlook: The company aims to complete its initial Business Combination by June 20, 2026, and may seek to extend the Combination Period if necessary, subject to shareholder approval and regulatory requirements.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company, a blank check entity, is focused on completing a Business Combination using proceeds from its Initial Public Offering and Private Placement Warrants. It aims to utilize cash, shares, and debt to effectuate this combination, with a strategic emphasis on identifying and evaluating target businesses for acquisition.
  • Capital Management: The company raised $160 million through its Initial Public Offering and an additional $5 million from the sale of Private Placement Warrants. These funds are held in a Trust Account and are intended for use in completing a Business Combination. The company has incurred $10.18 million in IPO-related costs and maintains $878,254 in cash for operational purposes. It has no long-term debt or off-balance sheet arrangements, and it has not paid any dividends, nor does it plan to before completing its Business Combination.
  • Future Outlook: The company has until June 20, 2026, to complete its initial Business Combination. If unsuccessful, it will undergo automatic winding up and liquidation. The company may need additional financing to complete the Business Combination or to redeem a significant number of public shares. It is also considering extending the Combination Period, which would require shareholder approval and could impact its Nasdaq listing.

Challenges and Risks

  • Business Challenges: The company is a blank check company with no revenue, which presents a significant challenge in evaluating its ability to select a suitable business target. The company has not initiated any substantive discussions with potential Business Combination targets, which adds uncertainty to its future operations. The focus is on the emerging finance sector, which includes retail finance, specialty finance, and financial technology, sectors that are rapidly evolving and may present both opportunities and risks.
  • Risk Factors: The company faces several risks as a blank check company, including the inability to select an appropriate target business within the Combination Period, which could lead to liquidation. There is a risk of not being able to retain or recruit key personnel post-Business Combination. The company may face conflicts of interest as officers and directors allocate their time between the company and other businesses. There is also a risk of not obtaining additional financing to complete the Business Combination or reduce shareholder redemptions. Market risks include the potential for an inactive market for public securities, leading to limited liquidity and trading. Regulatory risks include potential changes in laws or regulations that could adversely affect the company's ability to complete a Business Combination. The company may also face challenges due to recent fluctuations in inflation and interest rates, as well as geopolitical tensions that could impact market stability.
  • Management’s Discussion and Analysis: Management has not yet identified a target for the Business Combination, which adds to the uncertainty of future operations. The company plans to leverage its management team's extensive experience in emerging finance to identify high-growth investment opportunities. However, the lack of an established record of revenue and cash flows in potential target companies could negatively affect financial performance post-Business Combination.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks such as fluctuations in interest rates and inflation, which could impact the ability to consummate a Business Combination. Additionally, adverse developments in the financial services industry could affect the company's business prospects. The company also faces risks related to potential delisting from Nasdaq if it fails to meet the 36-Month Requirement, which could lead to reduced liquidity and trading of its securities.

SEC Filing: Melar Acquisition Corp. I/Cayman [ MACI ] - 10-K - Mar. 21, 2025

data.title: Communications Services Up as Risk Appetite Returns — Communications Services Roundup
data.description:
Communications services companies rose as traders rotated back into high-risk sectors.

Ticket resale platform StubHub filed for an initial public offering, quelling some concerns that a boom in live events had slowed.

Walt Disney recouped losses to finish up slightly on the week amid signs that a streaming bundle of Disney+ and Hulu was selling well.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: REG-GOGL – Changes to the Board composition
data.description:
Golden Ocean Group Limited (Nasdaq: GOGL & OSE: GOGL) (“GOGL” or the “Company”) is pleased to announce the appointment by the Board of Directors of Mr. Patrick De Brabandere and Mr. Patrick Molis as Directors of the Company.

Mr. Patrick De Brabandere holds a degree in Applied Economic Sciences from UCL Louvain-la Neuve. He started his career at the audit firm Arthur Andersen. In 1987, he joined Almabo, the former holding company of the Saverys family, as Project Controller. He became CFO of CMB NV in 1998 and was appointed director of CMB NV in 2002. In 2003, following the partial demerger of Exmar NV from CMB NV, he became director and CFO of Exmar NV, then COO. In 2020 he became CFO of Exmar NV again until June 2022. He currently is a director of CMB NV and he also sits on the Supervisory Board of CMB.TECH since 2023.

Mr. Patrick Molis graduated from the Institut d’Etudes Politiques de Paris and holds a Master’s degree in law from Paris X Nanterre. He started his career as a Magistrate at the Cour des Comptes after joining the National School of Administration. Mr. Patrick Molis was General Manager of Union Normande Investissement (1989-1992), CFO of Worms & Cie Group (1994-1997), General Manager of Compagnie Nationale de Navigation (1995- 1998), Chairman of the Board of Compagnie du Ponant (2012-2015) and Chairman and CEO of Héli-Union (2013-2022). He is currently Chairman of Compagnie Nationale de Navigation (since 1998) and director of Sabena Technics. He has previously served as member of the board of directors of Euronav Luxembourg (1995-2001), Euronav (2004-2010), Compagnie Maritime Nantaise (1995-2017), Compagnie Méridionale de Navigation (2008- 2022) and of the Conseil d’orientation du Domaine national de Chambord (2007-2017). Mr. Patrick Molis has been awarded the titles of Knight of the Legion of Honour and Officer of the Order of Merit. He currently sits on the Supervisory Board of CMB.TECH since 2023 as an independent member.

The Company further announces that Mr. Ola Lorentzon, Mr. John Fredriksen, Mr. Ben Mills and Mr. Cato Stonex in conjunction with the above appointments have stepped down from their positions as Directors of the Company. The Company would like to express its sincerest gratitude for the many years of positive contribution these directors have brought to the development of its business.

Taking into account these changes, the Board of Directors currently consists of Mr. James O’Shaughnessy, Ms. Tonesan Amissah, Mr. Patrick De Brabandere and Mr. Patrick Molis.

In its new composition, the Board of Directors looks forward to initiate conversations with the Company's new reference shareholder, CMB.TECH. The Board of Directors expects conversations to include potential strategic options, commercial cooperation, operational and technical partnerships, and/or other forms of business combinations. Until further notice, the Board of Directors intends to maintain the Company’s current dividend policy.

The Board of Directors

Golden Ocean Group Limited

Hamilton, Bermuda

March 21, 2025

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

Forward-looking statements: This release may contain certain forward-looking statements. By their nature, forward-looking statements involve risk and uncertainty because they reflect the Company’s current expectations and assumptions as to future events and circumstances that may not prove accurate. A number of material factors could cause actual results and developments to differ materially from those expressed or implied by these forward-looking statements.
